content.text:
You are classifying files for an AI-first external drive.

For each file object in the input JSON array, return a JSON array with one object per file.
Return valid JSON only. No markdown. No prose.

Each output object must contain:
- rel_path
- title
- content_kind
- domain
- lifecycle
- confidence
- tags: array of short strings
- summary: one sentence
- entities: array of objects with fields name and type
- relations: array of objects with fields from, relation, to
- recommended_zone
- canonical_priority: one of low, medium, high

Guidance:
- Infer semantics from path, filename, extension, and sampled text.
- Use practical domains like ai, security, infra, career, health, research, finance, media, prompt_engineering.
- Use lifecycle values only from: inbox, active, reference, archive, system.
- Keep entities and relations conservative; do not invent specifics unsupported by the input.
- Favor retrieval usefulness over literary summaries.
- Use recommended_zone only from: 00_inbox, 10_active, 20_reference, 30_archive, 40_system.
- Prefer 20_reference for documents meant to be reused, 10_active for live workspaces, 30_archive for dated snapshots and exports, 40_system for manifests/config/state, 00_inbox for ambiguous intake.

Input JSON:
[{"rel_path": "20_reference/knowledge_base/Product Development Request.pdf", "name": "Product Development Request.pdf", "extension": ".pdf", "mime_type": "application/pdf", "size_bytes": 47355, "zone": "20_reference", "sample_text": "Learn\u2011Your\u2011Way Codebase Analysis and\nImprovements\nProject structure and installed features\nThe provided archive expands to a Next.js/TypeScript project scaffolded around Z.ai. It includes a complete\nUI built with ShadCN components and hooks, a Prisma\u2013backed API layer, caching and retry helpers, and\nseveral skill modules (ASR, LLM, TTS, VLM, canvas\u2011design, document\u2011skills). The heart of the back\u2011end is an\nAPI route ( src/app/api/progress/route.ts ) which supports multiple actions \u2014 save\u2011progress ,\nget\u2011progress , get\u2011all\u2011progress , save\u2011preferences and get\u2011preferences \u2014 to persist user\nlearning progress via Prisma\u2019s upsert / findMany / findUnique methods\n\n1\n\n. The summary file also\n\nshows that an in\u2011memory caching layer ( lib/cache.ts ) and a comprehensive error\u2011handling module\n( lib/error\u2011handler.ts ) have been added to cut down expensive calls and provide retry logic with\nexponential backoff\n\n1\n\n.\n\nOn the front end, the UI offers a user settings page that loads/saves preferences from the API; a progress\ndashboard; a section\u2011quiz component with ARIA\u2011compliant markup, screen\u2011reader announcements and\nkeyboard shortcuts; and a hook ( use\u2011keyboard\u2011nav.ts ) that maps dozens of actions to"}]

thoughts.subject: Defining File Classifications
thoughts.description: I'm focused on the structure of the JSON output. I'm working through the specific fields each object will contain, making sure they align with the file characteristics provided. Currently, I'm considering how to best map the input data to the `title`, `content_kind`, and `domain` fields for optimal classification.

thoughts.subject: Dissecting the Input Data
thoughts.description: Okay, I've got a sample file to work with. Analyzing its fields, I'm concentrating on how `rel_path`, `name`, and `sample_text` can feed into the other fields. I'm focusing on deriving `title`, `content_kind`, and `domain` from these initial inputs. I'm now looking at extracting entities and relations to enrich the output.

thoughts.subject: Defining `content_kind`
thoughts.description: I've decided to categorize `content_kind` as "document" based on the `mime_type` "application/pdf." Thinking about how to refine this, I'm considering using the `sample_text` to distinguish between documents like "meeting notes" vs. a "requirements document". My next step will be to explore different classification levels within content kind.

thoughts.subject: Creating the Output JSON
thoughts.description: I'm now generating the JSON output based on the input file and my analysis. I'm focusing on structuring the array of objects, each representing a classified file. I'm filling out the fields: `rel_path`, `title`, `content_kind`, `domain`, `lifecycle`, `confidence`, `tags`, `summary`, and `entities`. Extracting `entities` and `relations` is still underway.

thoughts.subject: Generating the Output
thoughts.description: I've successfully mapped the input data to the required fields. Currently, I'm forming the final JSON structure for the single document. I'm especially focusing on populating `entities` and `relations` with relevant details, using the sample text to guide the extraction of key concepts and their connections. I've finished the classification, and now I will build a single JSON object for output.

thoughts.subject: Formulating the Output
thoughts.description: I'm now generating the JSON output structure. I've finished the classification of the fields based on the input document. I'm ready to populate the JSON array of objects with the derived information, including the `rel_path`, `title`, `content_kind`, `domain`, and others. Finally, I'll be extracting `entities` and `relations`.
